Node started: Compare the initial tree and the final tree to identify the specific set of 'merge' operations performed. A merge operation combines two sibling nodes into one. Identify which pairs of nodes were merged and which nodes were removed/replaced to reach the final state.

Node started: Given the set of identified merge operations, determine a valid execution sequence. A merge can only occur if the parent of the siblings involved has not been modified by a subsequent merge that changes the sibling structure in a way that invalidates the current operation. Ensure the sequence respects the bottom-up or top-down constraints inherent in tree transformations.
